Kigelia pinnata, commonly known as the Kinginda or African sausage tree, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Kigelia pinnata include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used to make a paste or a poultice, and is used to treat various ailments.
